meaning: adj.日趋衰弱的,苦思的,渴望的
examples: We don't spend much time on this topic today because we have been languishing on outdated ideas for too long.
With his popularity languishing near an all-time low, he may gain nothing from sticking firmly to his plans despite the howl of protests.
The soldier is lying on the bed, languishing of wounds.
Finally free from defending himself, Hurwitz could have walked away and focused on his languishing businesses.
Speaking of languishing plot lines, we also get a cursory scene between Stannis Baratheon and Melisandre.
